description: Rapid-fire idea generation without judgment — say everything that comes to mind, no filtering
Systematic creativity: Substitute, Combine, Adapt, Modify, Put to other use, Eliminate, Reverse
Reframe problems as opportunity questions starting with 'How might we...'
Use random words/images as creative triggers to force unexpected connections
Ask 'How could we make this WORSE?' then invert answers for solutions
Remove constraints: 'What if budget was unlimited?' 'What if time didn't matter?'
Each person gets 3-5 votes to place on favorite ideas — quick democratic prioritization
Plot ideas on 2x2: High Impact + Low Effort = Quick Wins (do first)
Cluster similar ideas together, name each group, look for themes
White=facts, Red=emotions, Yellow=benefits, Black=risks, Green=creativity, Blue=process
Ask 'Why?' 5 times to drill down to root cause — essential for problem definition
Strip away assumptions, rebuild from fundamental truths
Branch ideas from central concept — visual, spatial, connects related thoughts
List all constraints, classify as real vs assumed, find pathways around each
'This problem is like...' — transfer solutions from other domains
How would Elon Musk solve this? A 5-year-old? Your competitor?
How would this be solved in 1950? In 2050? In ancient Rome?
Generate intentionally bad ideas — often reveals hidden good ideas or clarifies constraints
